title: Conjecture 1.3 (Gruslys)
statement_text: Let $G$ be a finite vertex-transitive graph and let $H$ be an induced subgraph of $G$. If $|V(H)|$ divides $|V(G)|$, then there exists a positive integer $n$ such that $G^n$ admits a perfect induced $H$-packing.
context_text: Motivated by Gruslys's resolution of Offner's question on hypercube packings (Theorem 1.1) and the infinite tiling result of Gruslys, Leader and Tan (Theorem 1.2), Gruslys conjectured that the base divisibility condition suffices for any finite vertex-transitive host graph. The paper proves the conjecture for $G = C_k^m$ with $k$ even (Theorem 1.4) and disproves it in general by constructing counterexamples when $k$ is odd and not a prime power (Theorem 1.5).
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture when $k \geq 3$ is even (Theorem 1.4) and disproves it in general by showing that for every odd $k$ that is not a prime power there exist $m$ and an induced subgraph $H$ of $C_k^m$ with $|V(H)|$ dividing $k^m$ such that $C_k^n$ admits no perfect $H$-packing for any $n$ (Theorem 1.5).
paper_title: Partitioning the vertices of a torus into isomorphic subgraphs

title: Conjecture 1.6 (Gruslys, Leader and Tan)
statement_text: For $k \geq 1$, let $H$ be a non-empty subgraph of $Q_k$. Then there exists a positive integer $n$ such that the edges of $Q_n$ can be covered by edge-disjoint copies of $H$ (the copies of $H$ are not required to be induced).
context_text: Gruslys, Leader and Tan proposed this edge-packing analogue of Theorem 1.1 as a natural companion conjecture. The note that $|E(Q_n)| = n 2^{n-1}$ means the required divisibility condition on $|E(H)|$ is only satisfied for certain values of $n$, in contrast to the vertex-packing setting.
paper_contribution: Strongly disproves the conjecture by showing that for every $k \geq 5$ there exists a graph $H$ with $Q_{k-1} \subseteq H \subseteq Q_k$ such that no $n$ exists for which the edges of $Q_n$ can be covered by edge-disjoint copies of $H$ (Theorem 1.7).
paper_title: Partitioning the vertices of a torus into isomorphic subgraphs
